jobs.title: Sr Environmental Coordinator - Air Quality
jobs.description:
Job Description

The senior environmental coordinator works with operating unit personnel to ensure awareness of and compliance with environmental permits, regulations, policies and procedures and requirements of the company, assisting the operating group in the development of solutions to environmental issues. Monitors chemical, physical and biological hazards that could be present. Investigates environmental incidents and reports incidents to appropriate regulatory agencies. Assists with the development and delivery of environmental procedures and training. Implements environmental compliance self-inspections and recommends appropriate action to facility management. Recommends improvements in processes, design, procedures and operations to minimize environmental incidents. Assists in maintaining facilities’ environmental licenses, permits and authorizations. Maintains close contact with environmental health and safety, laboratory, operations and maintenance and other company departments. Assists in maintaining facilities’ environmental licenses, permits and authorizations. Responsibilities include submittal of all environmental reports in advance of deadlines including gathering operational information necessary to track, monitor and complete reporting requirements.

Responsibilities

Monitors Imperial Valley operations to assure compliance with federal, state and local regulations. Develops and maintains an understanding of processes within business units, and assists the business unit in complying with environmental aspects through the company’s environmental management systems. Identifies, develops and implements environmental initiatives for establishing external competitive advantages.
Investigates environmental issues and incidents and recommends steps to reduce the frequency of events and to resolve compliance issues. Recommends resolutions to integrate environmental assessments of facilities into facility operations to ensure compliance with environmental requirements.
Remains current on applicable environmental laws and regulations affecting the CalEnergy operations. Performs complex environmental work requiring considerable exercise of independent analysis and judgment in environmental services. Coordinates environmental activities of consulting firms.
Provides technical assistance to CalEnergy managers and supervisors. Maintains a specialized knowledge and expertise in air issues, and utilizes such knowledge in day-to-day functions.
Seeks and obtains required permits for operating the power operations for the CalEnergy facilities. Evaluates regulatory impact and develops environmental programs and processes. Provides technical input to assist in development of project planning.
Assists in development and delivery of environmental training and follows up with observations to ensure implementation and compliance with regulatory requirements.
Assists with development and implements environmental policies and procedures for the Imperial Valley operations.
Performs administrative activities necessary for the effective coordination with other department staff, including employee safety.
Performs any additional responsibilities as requested or assigned.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in chemistry, environmental sciences, engineering, or related field and three to five years of environmental experience or equivalent work experience. (Typically six years of related, progressive work experience would be needed for candidates applying for this position who do not possess a bachelor’s degree.)
Five years of related experience.
Background working in all environmental media. Demonstrated air quality environmental industry experience is required. A broad diversity of environmental work experience in private industry, governmental, and consulting is preferred.
In-depth knowledge of applicable governmental laws and regulations. Ability to review and summarize complex regulations, with the demonstrated ability to implement environmental programs.
Effective interpersonal skills and leadership abilities. Must demonstrate the ability to coordinate projects and work with others effectively in a diverse working environment.
Excellent written and oral communications skills.
Detail oriented. Excellent organization skills. Ability to prioritize environmental responsibilities.
Intermediate to advanced knowledge of personal computer software applications, including spreadsheet, word processing and database experience.
Ability to read, analyze and interpret documents such as business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, governmental regulations. Ability to write reports, business correspondence and procedure manuals. Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups of managers, clients, customers, the general public and employees at all levels.
Effective analytical, problem-solving and decision-making skills. Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as proportions, percentages, area, circumference and volume. Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry. Capable of drawing and interpreting graphs.
Ability to define obscure and incomplete problems collects data, establish facts and draw valid conclusions. Ability to conceptualize and develop creative alternatives to problem resolution. Ability to deal with abstract and concrete concepts.
Provides accurate input into the budget process and contributes to meeting the actual targets.
Attends work on a regular basis and supports the Company’s employee policies and procedures, including workplace safety rules.
A valid California driver’s license is required.

About Us

BHE Renewables delivers sustainable energy solutions for customers and communities. We own, operate and develop wind, solar, geothermal, hydroelectric and natural gas projects in 11 U.S. states. The power generated is sold into energy markets or directly to customers through long-term purchase agreements.
Headquartered in Des Moines, Iowa, BHE Renewables is an innovative and growing company with new offices in Phoenix and San Diego. Our 50/50 joint venture with Occidental Petroleum has led to a demonstration project showing how TerraLithium technology is used to extract lithium from the brine presently flowing through our geothermal plants in Imperial Valley, CA.
Our CHAMPION culture drives our success by fostering high expectations, ownership and continuous improvement within a respectful and inclusive team environment. Through the CHAMPION mindset, we focus on the fundamentals – preparation, planning and attention to detail – holding ourselves accountable to do simple better. Alongside this, the BHE Renewables CARES program encourages employee volunteerism and invests in our communities, reinforcing our commitment to excellence and collaboration, making us stronger together.

About the Team

BHE Renewables is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion or religious creed, age, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status (except as required by law), gender (including gender identity and expression), sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, genetic information, physical or mental disability, medical condition, veteran or military status, familial or parental status, marital status or any other category protected by applicable local, state or U.S. federal law. Employees must be able to perform the essential functions of the position, with or without an accommodation.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Services for Renewable Energy

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist – Contamination Specialist
jobs.description:
Environmental Scientist – Contamination Specialist

Location: Perth, WA

RPS, a Tetra Tech company, is a global professional services firm delivering sustainable solutions across the built and natural environment. With 5,600 people operating in 125 countries, we partner with clients to define, design and manage projects that create shared value in a complex, urbanising and resource-constrained world.

We are currently seeking a Contamination Specialist to join our Perth based Contaminated Land team. This is an excellent opportunity for an early-career professional looking to that next step in their career with the opportunity to further develop technical capability while taking responsibility for delivery of small to medium scale contamination assessments and remediation programs.

The opportunity

In this role, you will support the delivery of contamination investigation and remediation projects across a diverse client base. You will work alongside experienced professionals and play a hands-on role in both field and office-based activities.

Key responsibilities

You will contribute to a range of project activities, including:
Supporting contaminated site investigations and remediation programs
Assisting with HazMat surveys, registers and removal management plans
Conducting fieldwork including soil, groundwater, surface water, sediment and air sampling
Maintaining accurate field records, monitoring logs and managing analytical data (including Esdat)
Technical reporting and proposal preparation
Project management of small to medium sized assessment and remediation programs
Working both independently and collaboratively across project teams

About You

You are a motivated and adaptable environmental professional who enjoys working in a team and contributing to practical project outcomes.

You Will Bring

3–5 years’ experience in contaminated land or HazMat industries
A degree in Chemistry / Geology / Environmental Science or a related discipline
Experience in fieldwork and environmental sampling
Strong data interpretation and report writing skills
Familiarity with relevant WA and national guidelines (e.g. DWER 2021, DoH 2021, NEPM 2013, PFAS NEMP 2026)
An understanding of project delivery and health, safety and quality systems

Why join RPS, a Tetra Tech company?

We are committed to creating a supportive and high-performing environment where our people can thrive. You will have access to:

Professional development and career progression opportunities
A supportive team culture that celebrates collaboration and success
Flexible working arrangements to support work-life balance
Active wellbeing and community engagement initiatives
Annual professional membership reimbursement
Paid parental and partner leave
$250 active lifestyle allowance per annum
Additional annual leave purchase scheme and salary packaging options
Professional and technical development programs, including study assistance, online learning and mentoring across portfolios
Active office social events, employee assistance program, service recognition and more

Our approach

At RPS, we are easy to work with, confidently pragmatic and driven by a shared purpose to solve problems that matter. We value diverse perspectives and encourage applications from people of all backgrounds.

Our Approach

Our people live our purpose, deliver our promise, and demonstrate our behaviours every day. Our behaviours define who we are. Developed by our people, they underpin everything that we do. They create the memorable experiences we are known for and make RPS a great place to do great work.

Stronger together is one of our five core behaviours and it is key to building a collaborative workplace. We encourage everyone to bring their best and to support others to do the same. By sharing knowledge, challenging each other to think differently, and fostering an environment of trust and respect, our people can learn, grow and thrive – and share in our success.

Recruitment Agencies

We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Research, Analyst, and Information Technology

Industries

Business Consulting and Services
